name: Cova de l'Afortunat
starting_point: Upper part of the Costa d'en Blanes urbanization
description: Located near Cova des Ponent and connected via an impassable narrow conduit through which human bone remains were deposited in an undetermined era, adding archaeological interest. The lower level features collapse morphology with blocks and little decoration, while the upper level hosts abundant and varied speleothems with evidence of former standing water.
full_description:
It is located just a few meters from the entrance of the Cova des Ponent, with which it maintains a connection through a narrow impassable conduit. This opening was used in an undetermined era to dispose of human bone remains, a circumstance that adds evident archaeological interest to the cavity.

From a morphological point of view, the lower level presents a typology clearly associated with collapse processes, with a predominance of blocks and scarce presence of concretions. In contrast, the upper floor harbors a notable development of speleothems, with abundant and varied formations.

Access to the cave.

Upper level where abundant and varied speleothems are found.

The upper level shows signs of having held water in some small pool.
